channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Israel, Lebanon Hold Rare U.S.-Mediated Peace Talks on Hezbollah
item.description: But without the Iranian proxy group’s involvement, implementing a cease-fire will be difficult.

item.title: On Iran, China Softens Its Approach
item.description: Beijing’s relative quiet amid the Strait of Hormuz crisis underscores a domestic shift.

item.title: A tax revolt is under way in America
item.description: Republicans and Democrats alike want to take an axe to taxes

item.title: If it starts, a nuclear arms race will be unstoppable
item.description: A sobering interview with the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency

item.title: How to Navigate a Rogue America
item.description: Stephen M. Walt’s prescription for anxious countries.

item.title: Trump Always Skips the Hard Part
item.description: The U.S. president’s half-baked approach to dealmaking may be a recipe for more war.

item.title: Europe Is Looking for Its Own Hormuz Fix
item.description: The continent has relevant military strengths, but little appetite for fighting.

item.title: The Price Israel Is Paying for Its Wars
item.description: Fighting on multiple fronts has taxed its military, economy, and ties with the United States.

item.title: Keep Humans in the Loop
item.description: AI has a place in military targeting—but it needs safeguards.

item.title: How to build a portfolio when bonds fail to buffer stocks
item.description: The classic hedge has fallen apart, but don’t dump it just yet

item.title: A battle for female voters is changing India’s elections
item.description: Politicians are doling out cash to get their support

item.title: America’s corporate boards are under siege
item.description: Activist shareholders and regulatory changes are undermining their control

item.title: Japan Moves to Institutionalize Drone Warfare as Manpower Shortfalls Deepen
item.description: Two new GSDF offices signal a shift toward unmanned, AI-enabled operations amid demographic decline.

item.title: The Rural World Won’t Go Dry Without a Fight
item.description: From Jordan to Nepal, solutions to urban water woes are leaving the countryside angry and parched.

item.title: Why Are Educated Baloch Women Turning to Militancy?
item.description: The Corps Commander of Balochistan asked the question. He may not like the answer.

item.title: The Great Hungarian Reset
item.description: Now that he has ousted Viktor Orbán, incoming Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar inherits an economy burdened by a legacy of centralized mismanagement and systemic corruption. To address it, he will need a focused and pragmatic economic program.

item.title: The Hormuz Crisis and the Fate of the Global South
item.description: If the shock from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has revealed economic vulnerabilities, it has also illuminated stark differences in how countries absorb energy-price turbulence. Those that have invested in more resilient clean energy sources are faring better and offering lessons for everyone else.

item.title: Japan’s PM Faces Gas Tax Headwinds When She Flies to Australia
item.description: As Australia’s biggest gas customer, Japan is alarmed by growing calls for a new tax on gas exports. Elsewhere in Asia, leaders recognize renewables can help break reliance on volatile fossil fuel supply chains.

item.title: How to make buffet breakfasts less wasteful
item.description: A computer model has found some counterintuitive solutions

item.title: Buffet breakfasts could be less wasteful
item.description: A computer model has found some counterintuitive solutions

item.title: Understanding the Illegality of Trump’s Iran Threats
item.description: Destroying bridges and power plants would violate international law and expose service members to prosecution.

item.title: Why Israel continues to batter Lebanon
item.description: Even as the first official talks between the countries in decades are set to begin

item.title: An Opportunity or an Illusion? The Iran War and China’s Taiwan Calculus
item.description: The Iran war has shifted U.S. military capabilities out of the Indo-Pacific. Will Beijing try to take advantage?

item.title: Beyond Floppy Disk Economics
item.description: How to rewrite the global economic framework for a progressive multilateralism.

item.title: Afghanistan: Evolving Role of Regional Cooperation and UNAMA
item.description: Afghanistan is no longer viewed exclusively as a source of risks, but increasingly as a space of opportunities, especially from Central Asia’s perspective.

item.title: Coal Is Rising in China’s Clean Energy Transition
item.description: China is not moving from coal to renewables in a linear transition. Instead, it is attempting to expand both simultaneously.

item.title: Bangladesh’s Parties are Divided Over the Reform Process
item.description: Despite a clear "yes" vote in the referendum, the BNP government is dragging its feet, prompting the opposition to protest in parliament and the streets.

item.title: Why Development Doesn’t Prevent War
item.description: Violent conflicts have reached levels not seen since World War II, even as global poverty has fallen to historic lows, challenging long-held assumptions about the relationship between development and peace. This outcome calls for a reassessment of the theory of change that underpins development aid.

item.title: Russians Go Home
item.description: At a time when illiberalism has presented itself as the wave of the future, Hungarians just voted overwhelmingly to reverse course. But unlike the country’s liberation in 1989, this week’s electoral result represents only the beginning of a longer process, the outcome of which will remain uncertain.

item.title: Indian Navy Needs More Stringent Planning
item.description: The plan was to equip the navy with about 200 warships by 2027, but financial limitations dictate a more modest flotilla of just 170 vessels.

item.title: How Trump’s Crypto Push Is Undermining American Power
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s deregulation of cryptocurrency markets has introduced new financial risks and threatens to undermine the dollar’s global dominance. Russia, Iran, and North Korea have been the biggest beneficiaries, using crypto to evade American sanctions with impunity.

item.title: Thousands of Overseas Filipinos Celebrate Former President Duterte’s Birthday
item.description: Despite him facing charges of crimes against humanity at the ICC, support for the former Philippine leader remains strong among Filipinos abroad.

item.title: Southeast Asia’s Solar Panel Boom
item.description: It's not just about China. The world is now benefiting from historically cheap solar panels made in Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia.

item.title: Philippines Accuses China of Cyanide ‘Sabotage’ at South China Sea Shoal
item.description: Philippine security officials claim to have discovered plastic bottles of cyanide on Chinese boats close to Second Thomas Shoal in the Spratly Islands.

item.title: How Iran Helped Bring Ronald Reagan to Power
item.description: The hostage crisis dominated headlines—and left a lasting impression on Donald Trump.

item.title: A Test of Wills in Iran
item.description: Trump is still underestimating Tehran’s resolve.

item.title: How Congress Can Help Ukraine
item.description: The Taiwan Relations Act should serve as a model.

item.title: The Real Thucydides Trap
item.description: Overconfidence could draw America and China into a war.

item.title: Indonesia, US Announce New Defense Partnership as Prabowo Visits Russia
item.description: Washington and Jakarta have pledged to broaden and deepen their defense partnership, but Indonesia is unlikely to abandon its non-aligned foreign policy orientation.

item.title: The US and China Need to Talk About Unmanned Nukes
item.description: "Human-in-the-loop" is not enough.

item.title: Why Did Wang Yi Go to North Korea? China’s 3 Strategic Calculations
item.description: China had three interrelated goals in mind – all linked to the upcoming Trump-Xi summit in May.
